name: best-of-n-worktree
description: Stage-first best-of-N implementation recipe: isolated managed-worktree attempts with read-only comparison and human selection for the runtime task. No auto-merge.

tasks.prompt: Best-of-N attempt A. Implement the runtime task only inside this managed worktree. Keep the solution minimal and localized. Run relevant safe local checks. Do not merge, push, publish, deploy, or modify the main checkout. Finish with a concise summary including changed files, commands run, test results, known risks, and why this approach should or should not be selected.

Best-of-N attempt B. Implement the runtime task only inside this managed worktree using a deliberately different reasonable approach from attempt A when possible. Keep the solution minimal and localized. Run relevant safe local checks. Do not merge, push, publish, deploy, or modify the main checkout. Finish with a concise summary including changed files, commands run, test results, known risks, and why this approach should or should not be selected.

Best-of-N attempt C. Implement the runtime task only inside this managed worktree using the simplest conservative approach you can justify. Keep the solution minimal and localized. Run relevant safe local checks. Do not merge, push, publish, deploy, or modify the main checkout. Finish with a concise summary including changed files, commands run, test results, known risks, and why this approach should or should not be selected.

stages.prompt: Using Source Stage Context and the runtime task, compare the Best-of-N attempts. Treat attempt outputs as claims, not truth. Use provided worktree paths and output/result artifacts to inspect evidence when needed. Do not modify files. Do not merge anything. Recommend at most one winner, or recommend no winner if evidence is insufficient or all attempts are risky. Human selection is required before any merge back to the main checkout. Return Markdown with: Candidate summary table, Evidence checked, Recommended winner or no-winner, Merge/reject rationale, Human follow-up steps.
